title: Perf regression detection (dev/perf-history/ + perf-regression-check bin)
branch: main (local; unpushed) via ff from phase-0.7.2-PR-7-20260601T215338Z

commits.FIX-1: dda30b4 (codex pass-1 BLOCK x3 + honesty rework)
commits.FIX-2: e0a5919 (codex pass-2 BLOCK — strict RFC3339 value checks)

layout_choice.decision: (A) — [[bin]] perf-regression-check under fathomdb-cli; NO new workspace crate.
layout_choice.rationale: Lowest blast radius: touches only the CLI crate's manifest (added serde derive + the [[bin]] entry), no workspace-member edit, no version-axis churn (version.workspace=true inherited). Cargo.lock change is the serde wiring only.

thresholds_hitl_gate1.latest_definition: single most-recent run by parsed timestamp vs median of prior up-to-10 in group
thresholds_hitl_gate1.constants_location: perf-regression-check.rs — single named/commented block (LATENCY_DEGRADATION_FRACTION / RECALL_DEGRADATION_ABSOLUTE / ROLLING_WINDOW / MIN_RUNS_FOR_MEDIAN)
thresholds_hitl_gate1.rationale: Conservative pick (vs the 10%/0.02 handoff default). Recall sigma=0.0116 (PR-3) => 0.03 ~= 2.4 sigma ~= ~1% jitter false-flag rate. Codex's review focus is false-positive resistance. Tradeoff documented: a 0.03 drop from 0.937 lands at 0.907 (near the 0.90 floor) — favours FP-resistance over early-warning sensitivity; the hard floor is enforced by the AC-013b gate, not this drift detector.

bin_behavior.groups_by: (ac_id, n) — tier-aware; never cross-tier or real-vs-synthetic-N
bin_behavior.regime_caveat: K=64 (<=0.7.0) vs K=192 (>=0.7.1) latency NOT mixed in one group; the synthetic AC-013@10000 latency history begins at the K=192 PR-3 baseline. K=64 AC-013b@10000 recall arc kept as one internally-consistent group.
bin_behavior.timestamp: strict fixed-width RFC3339 -> UTC epoch at load (dependency-free parser); parsed epoch is the sort key (not lexical string); unparseable timestamp => exit 2; ties broken on commit_sha
bin_behavior.exit_codes: 0 = no regression (incl. all-insufficient-history); 1 = regression flagged; 2 = data missing/malformed (absent dir, bad JSON, no comparable metric, OR unparseable timestamp)
bin_behavior.output: human-readable default; --json (per-group verdicts + overall flagged bool)
bin_behavior.append_only: bin ONLY reads dev/perf-history/; never writes/mutates/deletes

backfill_scope.AC-012@1000000: 603a4bc0 p50=140.95/p99=458 (0.6.1 canonical run 26346417896, RED vs 20/150 ADR budget). Sole point => insufficient-history (honest RED preserved, not a flagged regression).
backfill_scope.AC-013b@10000 (K=64 arc, the only multi-: 035cfa30 recall=1.0 DEGENERATE (batch-collapse artifact, noted) -> 4a95cfd0 recall=0.1572 (honest post-fix, isotropic-sparsity pathology) -> 38f5e3a0 recall=0.5124 (dense fixture) -> 38d5f4f0 recall=0.5124 (v0.7.0 ship). Latest healthy => committed group CLEAN.
backfill_scope.AC-013@10000: c893d8b0 p50=15/p99=17 (PR-3 synthetic 384-d, K=192). Latency-only. Insufficient-history.
backfill_scope.AC-013@7667 / AC-013b@7667 / AC-019@7667: c893d8b0 real bge: latency 36/49, recall 0.937, stress p99 343. AC-013 latency-only, AC-013b recall-only. Each insufficient-history (forward baseline).
backfill_scope.honest_state: Most groups are single-point (insufficient-history) — the real consequence of sparse once-per-release cadence + K-regime + synthetic->real transitions. Store accrues comparability going forward.

batch_collapse_honesty.finding: The 2026-05-27 batch-collapse bug masqueraded as an IMPROVEMENT (index collapsed to ~10 vec0 rows => recall falsely 1.0, latency anomalously fast). A degradation-only detector (the HITL-locked design) STRUCTURALLY cannot flag the bug itself — it flags the regression-shaped CORRECTION after the fix (recall 1.0 -> 0.1572, a 0.84 drop at commit 4a95cfd).
batch_collapse_honesty.implementer_error_corrected: The original GREEN backfill mis-attributed the numbers (modeled 035cfa3 as a 0.1572 'drop' and 4a95cfd as 0.5124 recovery). Real numbers per STATUS-perf-vector-quant.md: collapse read degenerate 1.0; 0.1572 is the honest post-fix isotropic number; 0.5124 is the later dense fixture.
batch_collapse_honesty.future_work: Catching the bug ITSELF needs a row-count / vec0-population invariant or an implausible-perfection anomaly rule (recall==1.0, latency falling as N rises). Deliberately out of scope here (would expand past the locked 15%/0.03 thresholds + risk FPs on legitimately-perfect small-N recall). Logged in the design doc.

tests.bin_unit_rfc3339: 5/5 (utc epoch, offset normalization, arc ordering, valid edge cases incl. leap day/fractional/leap second, reject set incl. impossible date / out-of-range offset / bad fraction / non-canonical widths)

fixtures.jitter: normal noise within thresholds -> exit 0 (false-positive proof)
fixtures.backfill-dirty: degenerate 1.0 @035cfa3 -> honest 0.1572 @4a95cfd -> exit 1, flags the correction at 4a95cfd (recall 0.8428 below median 1.0)
fixtures.insufficient: 1 run in a group -> exit 0, 'insufficient' note

tests.committed_store: exit 0 (clean); AC-013b@10000 arc present (4 runs) and healthy at latest

ci_integration.removed: the dead PR-comment github-script step + the unused pull-requests: write permission (workflow has no PR/push event to comment on)
ci_integration.exit_handling: exit 1 -> step continues (summary is the signal); exit 2 -> ::error:: + non-zero (data-integrity fails loudly)

codex_review.reviewer: codex gpt-5.4, codex exec --sandbox read-only, model_reasoning_effort=high
codex_review.arc: BLOCK -> BLOCK -> BLOCK -> PASS (no BLOCK overridden)
codex_review.pass1_BLOCK: backfill chronology inverted; lexical-sorted unparsed timestamps; CI comment dead on workflow_dispatch + pull-requests:write over-grant
codex_review.pass2_BLOCK: parser not strict — impossible dates normalized, unbounded offsets, unvalidated fractional suffix
codex_review.pass3_BLOCK: parser accepted non-canonical field widths (1-digit fields, 5-digit year)

honesty_notes: RED commit 60878e9 was authored before GREEN (7/10 failing against a stub).

The GREEN backfill had a real honesty defect (batch-collapse numbers mis-attributed + inverted chronology) caught by codex pass-1 + main-thread cross-check against STATUS-perf-vector-quant.md; corrected under HITL option A. Recorded rather than smoothed.
Most committed groups are insufficient-history — surfaced honestly, not padded with fabricated comparison points.

The detector cannot flag the batch-collapse bug itself (it looked like an improvement); it flags the correction. Stated plainly in the design doc + README; the true bug-catch is logged as future work.
push: NONE — campaign-wide no-push without HITL OK; PR-8 owns the 0.7.2 closure push.
pointer_forward: PR-6 (dev-loop perf gates) is the remaining Phase-B sibling; then PR-8 (campaign closure + 0.7.2 push).
